agency: Office of the Bronx Borough President
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA; the site serves no real robots.txt — the path returns a 404 CMS page). Fingerprinted bronxboropres.nyc.gov (resolves to an Oracle Cloud IP, 129.153.208.86) as a Revize government SaaS CMS: PHP front end (index.php, news_detail_T4_R<n>.php, calendar.php), Revize plugins (revize_calendar importing two Google Calendars 'Events'+'Meetings'; document_center serving ULURP PDFs), Bootstrap 4.6 + jQuery 3.7.1, with a JSP CMS admin at cms2.revize.com/revize/security. Third parties observed: Curator.io social wall, Constant Contact newsletter (enotify), UserWay accessibility widget, Google Tag Manager / GA4 (G-DMD6JC0H5S), Font Awesome. The nyc.gov/site/bronxbp path 404s — the office is NOT on the shared NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform. Verified NYC Open Data: the agency label is 'Bronx Borough President (BPBX)' and returns exactly 2 datasets, both pulled with columns.
headline_findings: The Bronx Borough President site (bronxboropres.nyc.gov) is a thin brochure site on the Revize government SaaS CMS — PHP front end, JSP CMS back end, hosted on Oracle Cloud, NOT on the shared NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform. It exposes no API and no OpenAPI.

Only TWO of the office's entities are machine-readable at all — as NYC Open Data datasets under the label 'Bronx Borough President (BPBX)': Bronx Borough President Capital Funding (mdgu-ar69, 20 columns) and Bronx Community Boards (wbau-xy7g, 7 columns).

The office's charter-mandated work is otherwise trapped in unstructured surfaces: ULURP land-use recommendations are PDFs in a Revize 'document center', the newsroom (releases, statements, letters, testimonies) is server-rendered PHP pages, and events live in two imported Google Calendars.

Constituent-facing intake is analog: applying to serve on a community board is a downloadable PDF / email; there is no structured application and no way to check status. That is the net-new WRITE surface.

All FIVE Borough President offices carry the same NYC Charter duties (ULURP advisory recommendations, community-board appointments, a share of discretionary capital/expense funding, chairing the borough board) and run near-identical thin sites on assorted vendor CMSes. This is a candidate for ONE shared, templatable Borough President API instantiated per borough — not five bespoke builds. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: Exactly 2 datasets under 'Bronx Borough President (BPBX)': Capital Funding (mdgu-ar69) and Bronx Community Boards (wbau-xy7g). SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The only real, open API surface this office has.

apis_observed.owner: Office of the Bronx BP (on Revize SaaS)
apis_observed.note: PHP front end (index.php, news_detail_T4_R<n>.php, calendar.php), Revize document_center (ULURP PDFs) + revize_calendar plugins; JSP CMS admin at cms2.revize.com/revize/security. No JSON/OpenAPI. Hosted on Oracle Cloud (129.153.208.86).
Two imported calendars — 'Events' and 'Meetings' — surfaced through the Revize calendar plugin on calendar.php. Not an owned API.
Social wall embedded on the homepage.
GA4 analytics and the UserWay accessibility overlay.

fruit.name: Discretionary Funding Awards (Capital Funding)
opendata_match: Bronx Borough President Capital Funding mdgu-ar69 (20c)
fruit.notes: The BP's share of city budget directed to organizations/projects — Organization, Purpose, Funding, Type, Fiscal Year, Project Location + full NYC geography spine. One of only two machine-readable entities.

fruit.name: Community Board Appointments
fruit.notes: Members appointed to the twelve Bronx community boards — First/Last name, Community Board, Recommending Official, Term expires, Year, Zip Code of Application. A charter duty of the BP. One of only two machine-readable entities.

fruit.name: Land-Use / ULURP Recommendations
fruit.notes: The BP's charter-mandated written advisory recommendations on every ULURP application in the borough — published only as PDFs in a Revize 'document center' (315 document_center references, 40+ PDFs on the page). No Open Data twin, no API.

fruit.name: Newsroom (Releases, Statements, Letters, Testimonies)
fruit.notes: Dozens of news_detail_T4_R<n>.php pages (Releases & Statements, Letters & Testimonies, Videos). No feed, no API, no machine-readable shape.

fruit.name: Events & Meetings
fruit.notes: Two Google Calendars ('Events' and 'Meetings') surfaced via the Revize calendar plugin. No owned API; the office does not control the contract.

fruit.name: Apply to a Community Board
fruit.notes: Residents apply to serve on a community board via a downloadable PDF / email — no structured intake, no confirmation, no status check. The net-new WRITE surface; the natural inbound counterpart to the published appointment data (wbau-xy7g even records the applicant's 'Zip Code of Application').

fruit.name: Constituent Services / Advisory Councils
fruit.notes: Constituent help, plus advisory councils & task forces (African Advisory Council, Disability Task Force, Veterans, LGBTQIA+, NYCHA, Muslim Leadership Roundtable). All static pages — no intake, no data. A second candidate for the net-new write surface (a generic constituent request).

meta.platform: Revize government SaaS CMS (PHP front end + JSP admin at cms2.revize.com), on Oracle Cloud; NOT the shared NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform. Events on Google Calendar; Curator.io + Constant Contact + UserWay + GA4 widgets.
meta.netnew: CommunityBoardApplication — apply to serve on one of the Bronx's twelve community boards (today a PDF/email; no structured intake or status).
meta.tagline: A thin brochure site on a vendor CMS where only two of the office's charter entities are machine-readable — and where the fix is one shared Borough President API, not a bespoke Bronx build.
meta.headline: The Bronx Borough President runs a Revize brochure site with no API and just two Open Data datasets; because all five BP offices share the same charter duties, the low-hanging fruit is one templatable Borough President API.

scorecard_notes.platform: Proprietary Revize SaaS CMS (PHP/JSP) on Oracle Cloud; managed but wholly vendor-owned, no API surface.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: No site API at all — no JSON, no OpenAPI; content is PHP pages + document-center PDFs.
scorecard_notes.openData: Exactly two small Socrata datasets (Capital Funding, Community Boards) — real but thin.
scorecard_notes.coreData: Two of ~six core entities are machine-readable; ULURP recommendations, newsroom, and events are not.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: None — applying to a community board or requesting help is a PDF/email; no structured intake.
scorecard_notes.agent: No agent-native surface of any kind.
scorecard_notes.ownership: Site content locked in a vendor CMS; events in Google Calendar, social in Curator.io, email in Constant Contact — only the two Open Data datasets sit on city-owned infrastructure.
